---
title: "10 — Advanced Visualization (gganimate, maps)"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(ggplot2)
```

## gganimate — animated ggplot2

```{r eval=FALSE}
library(gganimate)

# transition_states — discrete states (e.g. year)
p <- ggplot(gapminder, aes(gdpPercap, lifeExp, size = pop, colour = continent)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  transition_states(year, transition_length = 2, state_length = 1) +
  labs(title = "Year: {closest_state}")

animate(p, nframes = 100, fps = 10)
anim_save("gapminder.gif")
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# transition_time — continuous time variable
ggplot(data, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_point() +
  transition_time(time) +
  ease_aes("linear") # easing: linear, cubic-in-out, bounce, etc.
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# frame aesthetic (older gganimate API / ggplotly)
ggplot(data, aes(x, y, frame = year)) +
  geom_point()
ggplotly() # makes it interactive with plotly
```

## Interactive: plotly

```{r eval=FALSE}
library(plotly)
p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point()
ggplotly(p) # converts ggplot to interactive HTML widget
```

## Maps — sf package

```{r eval=FALSE}
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)

# Read shapefile / geojson
world <- st_read("world.shp")

# Choropleth map
ggplot(world) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = gdp_per_cap)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Join spatial data with attribute data
merged <- world |> left_join(data, by = c("iso_a3" = "code"))
ggplot(merged) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = value))
```

## Maps — geom_polygon (base approach)

```{r eval=FALSE}
library(maps)
world_map <- map_data("world")

ggplot(world_map, aes(long, lat, group = group)) +
  geom_polygon(fill = "lightgray", colour = "white") +
  coord_fixed(1.3)
```

## Labels — ggrepel

```{r eval=FALSE}
library(ggrepel)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, label = rownames(mtcars))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text_repel() # non-overlapping labels
```

## Hurricane wind radii — wide → long pattern

```{r eval=FALSE}
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# 12 wind-radius columns → long format
hur_long <- hur |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = ts_radius_NE:r64_NW,
    names_to  = "quadrant",
    values_to = "radius"
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(radius), radius > 0) |>
  separate(quadrant, into = c("threshold", "direction"), sep = "_(?=[A-Z]{2}$)")

# Plot wind radii as a rose
ggplot(
  hur_long |> filter(storm_id == "AL0188", threshold == "ts_radius"),
  aes(x = direction, y = radius, fill = direction)
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_polar()
```
